key_highlights: Markets hit record highs on US-Iran negotiation optimism, with S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 closing at all-time highs.
Intel surged on strong Q1 earnings and outlook, driving semiconductor sector gains.
Iran war remains the dominant macro risk, with energy security at an all-time high and oil prices volatile.
Meta announced 8,000 job cuts while gaining analyst support on AI and ad strength.
DOJ dropped investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Powell, clearing path for Kevin Warsh as next Fed chair.
Gold and Treasuries rallied on safe-haven demand amid geopolitical tensions.
Cannabis sector saw a major catalyst as CB1 Capital Management called eased restrictions the biggest news since Nixon.
Nike cut 1,400 jobs in second layoff round this year, signaling consumer weakness.
Amazon-backed X-Energy surged 27% in its $1.02B IPO, highlighting nuclear energy interest.
Private credit and insurance sectors face scrutiny over risk transfer and trust gaps.

sectors.trend: Mixed but broadly positive. Intel's blowout earnings and strong outlook drove semiconductor re-rating, while IBM tanked on AI concerns. Meta and Microsoft gained analyst support on AI and cloud growth. Super Micro Computer plunged on lost Oracle contract and supply chain issues.
sectors.implications: AI remains the dominant theme but differentiation is key. Companies with clear AI monetization (Intel, Microsoft, Meta) are rewarded; those with execution risk (IBM, SMCI) are punished. Semiconductor demand for AI servers remains robust.

sectors.trend: Extreme volatility. Oil prices surged on Iran war impact but declined on renewed negotiation hopes. IEA called it the biggest ever energy security threat. Brazil raised ethanol blend to mitigate fuel impact. Military-grade jet fuel cargoes from US highlight war scale.
sectors.implications: Energy security is paramount. Non-conflict oil stocks (XOP) are being re-rated. Alternative energy and nuclear (X-Energy IPO) gain attention. Short-term oil price spikes likely on any escalation, but negotiations provide downside risk.

sectors.trend: Strong demand amid Iran war. Pentagon continues to call on defense contractors. Boeing analyst report positive. Kratos and other defense tech stocks highlighted as upside plays.
sectors.implications: Defense spending is structurally higher. Long-term contracts and recurring revenue from munitions, drones, and cyber are attractive. Investors should focus on companies with direct Pentagon exposure.

sectors.trend: Weakness evident. Nike cut 1,400 jobs in second layoff round. JetBlue sued for using private customer data to set prices. Spirit Airlines nears $500M bailout. Avis short squeeze collapsed 70%.
sectors.implications: Consumer spending is under pressure from inflation and geopolitical uncertainty. Airlines and retail face margin compression. Selective opportunities in resilient brands (Lululemon vs Nike) and travel recovery plays.

sectors.trend: Mixed. Citadel halts NYC expansion, signaling caution. KKR expected to grow >20% P.A. JPMorgan warns on safety moves. Private credit needs to close trust gap. Capital One settles for $425M. Goldman avoids charity cases in ETF market-making.
sectors.implications: Large banks are cautious on expansion. Private credit and alternative asset managers (KKR) offer growth but face regulatory and trust headwinds. Insurance sector shifting risk offshore raises systemic concerns.

sectors.trend: Positive catalyst from FDA approval of first-ever gene therapy to restore hearing. UnitedHealth Group called a buy by Jim Cramer. Praxis Precision Medicines highlighted as upside stock.
sectors.implications: Gene therapy breakthrough opens new frontier. Managed care (UnitedHealth) remains defensive with strong fundamentals. Biotech upside plays require careful due diligence.

sectors.trend: Major regulatory catalyst. CB1 Capital Management says eased restrictions are biggest news since Nixon. Aurora Cannabis analyst bullish.
sectors.implications: US federal rescheduling or decriminalization would unlock massive market. Canadian LPs (Aurora) and US MSOs could see significant re-rating. High risk but asymmetric upside.

sectors.trend: Crisis rhetoric. VantageScore CEO says mortgage and housing industry in crisis. Masco Corp analyst report mixed. Churchill Downs acquires Preakness IP.
sectors.implications: High rates and affordability crisis persist. Home improvement (Masco) may face headwinds. Commercial real estate stress continues. Selective opportunities in data centers and infrastructure.

key_themes: Geopolitical risk: Iran war and energy security dominate macro narrative.
AI monetization divergence: Winners (Intel, Microsoft, Meta) vs losers (IBM, SMCI).
Central bank transition: DOJ drops Powell probe, Warsh signals regime change.
Regulatory shifts: Cannabis easing, prediction markets (Kalshi, Polymarket) face scrutiny.
Consumer strain: Layoffs (Nike, Meta), airline bailouts, short squeeze collapses.
Defense spending surge: Pentagon demand for contractors in Iran war era.
Nuclear energy renaissance: X-Energy IPO success highlights investor appetite.
Private credit and insurance risk: Trust gaps and offshore risk transfer.
Gene therapy breakthrough: FDA approval for hearing restoration.
China exposure: Foreign car companies use tech to hold onto China market; US sanctions China refinery.

risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase exposure to non-conflict oil stocks (XOP), defense contractors, and energy infrastructure. Hedge with gold and Treasuries. Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary and airlines.
Focus on companies with proven AI monetization (MSFT, META, INTC). Avoid names with execution risk (IBM, SMCI). Diversify across AI infrastructure, software, and semiconductor supply chain.
Reduce exposure to discretionary retail (NKE) and airlines (SAVE). Favor defensive consumer staples and healthcare. Monitor job market data and consumer confidence.
Prepare for potential regime change under Warsh (hawkish). Lock in yields on Treasuries. Favor floating rate debt and inflation-protected securities. Monitor Fed communication closely.
Reduce exposure to high-yield and private credit. Favor investment-grade bonds. Monitor insurance sector offshore risk transfer. Barings notes 'pockets of complacency' in credit.
Avoid companies with significant regulatory exposure (cannabis, prediction markets, airlines). Favor companies with clean compliance records. Monitor DOJ and SEC actions.
Diversify across geographies and suppliers. Favor companies with nearshoring and automation. Monitor Panama Canal and Strait of Hormuz chokepoints.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Intel (INTC) on strong Q1 beat and server CPU growth re-rating
opportunities.rationale: Intel's earnings beat and outlook, combined with HSBC backing and multi-billion dollar bond sale, signal a turnaround. AI-driven server demand is a key catalyst.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase exposure to non-conflict oil stocks via XOP
opportunities.rationale: XOP is re-rating as investors seek oil exposure without Iran war risk. The fund focuses on US E&P companies with low geopolitical risk.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Microsoft (MSFT) on AI and Azure growth
opportunities.rationale: Analyst support for Copilot and Azure growth, combined with strong AI demand, makes MSFT a core holding. OpenAI's GPT-5.5 release further validates the ecosystem.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy defense contractors with Pentagon exposure (KTOS, BA)
opportunities.rationale: Iran war era drives sustained defense spending. Kratos (KTOS) and Boeing (BA) are well-positioned for long-term contracts.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy gold on safe-haven demand and Fed transition
opportunities.rationale: Gold gains on Iran talks optimism and Fed outlook. Safe-haven demand remains strong amid geopolitical uncertainty and potential policy regime change.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy cannabis stocks on regulatory easing catalyst
opportunities.rationale: CB1 Capital Management calls eased restrictions biggest news since Nixon. Aurora Cannabis (ACB) and US MSOs could see significant upside if federal rescheduling occurs.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy TSMC (TSM) on pricing power and strong earnings
opportunities.rationale: Barclays raised target after strong earnings. TSMC holds underappreciated pricing power in semiconductor manufacturing. AI demand is a structural tailwind.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase allocation to Treasuries and investment-grade bonds
defensive_moves.rationale: Treasuries gain on DOJ dropping Fed probe and safe-haven demand. Lock in yields before potential Warsh hawkish shift. Barings warns of complacency in credit.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary and airlines
defensive_moves.rationale: Nike layoffs, JetBlue lawsuit, Spirit bailout, and Avis short squeeze collapse indicate consumer strain. Avoid NKE, SAVE, JBLU, CAR.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against oil price spikes with energy sector exposure
defensive_moves.rationale: Iran war creates biggest ever energy security threat. Oil prices volatile but could spike on any escalation. XOP and US energy stocks provide hedge.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to private credit and high-yield bonds
defensive_moves.rationale: Private credit needs to close trust gap. Insurance sector shifting risk offshore. JPMorgan warns on safety moves. Favor investment-grade credit.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash position to 10-15% for tactical opportunities
defensive_moves.rationale: Market at record highs with significant geopolitical risk. Cash provides optionality to deploy during pullbacks. JPMorgan warns investors moving to safety.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets are likely to remain volatile with a positive bias, driven by US-Iran negotiation progress and strong tech earnings. However, any escalation in the Iran war could trigger sharp risk-off moves. Oil prices will be the key swing factor. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 are at record highs, suggesting momentum, but breadth is narrow. Expect consolidation or a pullback of 3-5% in the next 1-3 months.
market_outlook.long_term: The long-term outlook is cautiously optimistic. AI-driven productivity gains, defense spending, and energy security investments provide structural growth. However, the Iran war, consumer strain, and central bank transition (Warsh) pose risks. Inflation may remain sticky due to energy costs. A 6-12 month view favors sectors with pricing power (semiconductors, defense, energy) and defensive positioning (Treasuries, gold). The cannabis sector could be a major outperformer if federal easing materializes.
key_catalysts: US-Iran negotiation outcomes and potential ceasefire
Fed chair transition from Powell to Warsh and policy signals
Q1 2026 earnings season: Tech (MSFT, META, AMZN, GOOGL) and energy reports
FDA gene therapy approval impact on biotech sector
Cannabis federal rescheduling or decriminalization progress
Consumer spending data (retail sales, jobless claims, consumer confidence)
Oil price movements and Strait of Hormuz chokepoint developments
DOJ and SEC regulatory actions on prediction markets and insider trading
Nuclear energy and AI infrastructure investment announcements
watch_list: WTI and Brent crude oil prices
Intel (INTC) and Nvidia (NVDA) stock performance as AI bellwethers
Iran war headlines and diplomatic communiques
Private credit ETF (PSC) and high-yield spreads
Panama Canal transit fees and shipping costs
